insight: Valkyrion is considered weak in a vacuum (2 attack plus 2 Gauss charges for a high tech cost) but potentially very strong with synergy units such as Bloodrager, Iso Kronus, Thermite, Lancetooth, and Cryo Ray. Most community experts agreed it looked broken before playtesting but felt fine after.
author: consensus
context: Community discussion on the day Valkyrion was revealed as part of a balance patch, with multiple experts weighing in on its strength.

insight: Valkyrion's key strategic value is denying absorb (both regular and barrier absorb) rather than pure damage output. Denying 4 absorb is considered better than dealing 2 damage, and the interaction with Thermite is particularly degenerate because you need 2 Thermites to anti-absorb a Valkyrion, leaving barriers that do nothing.
author: redrame
context: Discussion of Valkyrion's synergy power and specifically the Thermite interaction on its patch release day.

insight: Expert ranking of Valkyrion's most degenerate synergies (from most to least problematic): Thermite > Cryo Ray > Lancetooth > Bloodrager. Arka Valkyrion was also noted as strong. The combination of Cryo Ray, Thermite, and Valkyrion together was described as outright breaking the game.
author: redrame
context: Experts debating which Valkyrion synergies were most problematic shortly after patch release.

insight: Valkyrion has value in holding back to block (similar to Lancetooth), exploiting 1-granularity absorb denial, and syncing attacks — making it a unit that requires significant Prismata skill to use optimally, not just a simple click-every-turn attacker.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of how difficult Valkyrion is to pilot correctly versus how simple it appears on the surface.

insight: Smorcus's primary strategic strength is that it allows large-unit efficiency with small, incremental purchases: you can buy Smorcus first and add Animus later to begin clicking it, decoupling the tech investment from the unit purchase.
author: .bky_1556
context: Discussion of a newly revealed red attacker unit (Smorcus) and why it is stronger than it first appears.

insight: Mobile Animus (MA) is particularly strong with Amplifuge (ampo) because it lets the ampo player convert unused red into Rhinos for emergency defense on the same turn or immediately after the Amplifuge purchase, potentially getting 3 Rhinos instead of 2 after a 13-drone ampo.
author: extratricky
context: Community analysis of Mobile Animus use cases on its reveal day, focusing on its synergy with Amplifuge builds.

insight: Mobile Animus's click ability (spending the unit to produce a Rhino) is best used late in the game when you no longer need the red production, or in sets where you only ever wanted red for a single large unit. Clicking and re-buying MA is the equivalent of paying 7 gold for a Rhino when MA is your red source.
author: consensus
context: Thread discussion evaluating when it is correct to click Mobile Animus versus retaining it for red production.

insight: Mobile Animus can replace a dedicated Animus in natural-conduit-style builds when a player only needs red production once or for a single large unit (e.g., Wincer, Antima Comet, Centurion). Buying two Mobile Animus over one Animus is rarer because most single-red units only need 1R.
author: mtanzer
context: Discussion of Mobile Animus use cases and when 2xMA is preferable to 1 Animus.

insight: The Savior HP nerf (losing fragile and having HP reduced) was considered meaningful specifically in matchups versus Tia Threnody and Grenade Mech, where Savior could previously survive a breach of 4-5 damage and recover by buying drones. With the nerf, previously safe underdefending by 4-5 is no longer viable with support units like Endotherm Kit, Wincer, or Arka.
author: redrame
context: Experts evaluating the impact of the April 2018 Savior nerf on specific matchups.

insight: Zemora Voidbringer (old 'fuge') was typically P2-favored or roughly balanced in the old version. The redesigned new Valkyrion-era Zemora was assessed as slightly P1-favored because it fits naturally into P1's build timing (similar to how blue sets favor P1 because Blastforge fits P1's build better), though the effect is not large.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of player-position advantage with Zemora Voidbringer after the April 2018 redesign.

insight: Player-position (coinflip) advantage matters in roughly 10-20% of games at high level, and when it does matter it is almost always the same small set of repeatedly problematic units causing it. Expert estimate is that coinflip does not detectably affect outcomes in 80-90% of games, though cognitive bias makes it feel more impactful.
author: redrame
context: High-level discussion prompted by a newer player questioning how much P1/P2 position matters.

insight: Valkyrion's Rhino-building ability does not cost the red resource, meaning the red produced can still be used for attack (shock/burst), giving Valkyrion an additional layer of resource efficiency compared to units that consume red when building defense.
author: .bky_1556
context: Clarification of Valkyrion's mechanics during its reveal discussion.
